commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Favour. Mercy would then be contrary to justice. (Psalm lxxi. 2; St. Augustine q. 88.)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Neither shall you be partial to a poor man in his cause. “You shall not favor a poor man in his lawsuit,” Scripture says. What therefore is the meaning of these words. “Do not be overcome by pity or unduly influenced if the wrongdoer happens to be a poor man,” it means. And if we must not show favor to the poor man, much more must we not do so for the rich. Moreover, I address these words not only to judges but also to all men, so that justice may nowhere be corrupted but everywhere kept inviolate.
